utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good afternoon , beverly . good to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nice to see you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i know my nurse told you a little bit about dax . um , now , if you do n't mind , i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: go for it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . um , beverly's a 24 year old female patient , and i know you have a history of type 2 diabetes and high blood pressure . correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , you're here today for your yearly exam . have n't seen you in a year . how're you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been doing well . i began working in a bakery this summer which has been super fun. i have really been enjoying working there, but i find myself, since i am so close to so many delicious treats, snacking a lot during shifts and eating a lot more desserts than i normally would.

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so , that brings me to your diabetes . so , how have your blood sugars been ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , not so good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , they've been up and down because of my diet , i think , because i've been taking my medication as prescribed . um , so , i have n't missed anything there , but my diet has been pretty bad lately .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: pretty bad lately ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , that's really the only thing that's different is your diet , pretty much ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and , that's because of the new job and all that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah. it has been really challenging to resist the temptation.

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's hard to ignore those desserts , are n't they ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no willpower like me ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no willpower .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and , and then , um , i know you've had high blood pressure , hypertension . how is , how have your blood sugars been ? have you been checking them ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , my blood pressure's been good . um , despite my , um , my diabetes levels being up and down .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , that's been good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so , your blood pressures have been normal . you've been sleeping well ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep , getting a full eight hours .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no concerns about hurting yourself or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . okay , and i know my , um , nurse did a review of systems . other than what we've talked about so far , is there anything else that you needed to add ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . so , pretty much status quo except for those things that we've been working on , right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . all right . well , let me do a quick physical exam , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , squeeze my finger here . good . can you feel me touch out here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , can you feel me touch you down here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . no swelling . push your leg out . good . pull it back . mm-hmm . go . good . okay . so , on my exam , um , essentially , your exam is normal . your , um , heart is regular . you do have that grade 2 , um , systolic ejection fraction back ejection murmur that you , um , that we've heard in the past . okay ? so , um , we definitely know that's there . that's unchanged , okay ? um , your lungs are clear . um , your grips are equal . neurovascular's intact . you do n't have any carotid bruits in your neck , no thyromegaly . no edema . um , pulses are good , so essentially , your exam is , is essentially normal . let's take a look at some of your lab work , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the hemoglobin a1c . okay , so , blood sugars have been elevated . your hemoglobin a1c is elevated as well , so that tells me your blood sugars have been a little bit out of control . you're still taking the metformin , correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and you take that twice a day ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , uh , hey , dragon ? show me the vital signs . okay , good news here is your blood pressure's good . everything , um , there is fine . so , um , so , my assessment is , is , um , your first problem is diabetes . it's a little out of control . we'd like that hemoglobin a1c to be under seven , um , at least . you know , preferably , under six .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , um , uh , i'm not gon na change your medication . i think this is diet related , so i want you to try to cut down on the snacking and additional sweets , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and , i want you to check your blood sugars in the morning . i want you to call them in in about two weeks so we know what your first , um , fasting blood sugar was in the morning , and then , we'll come back and see you in about two months and see if everything has straightened out . if it has n't , then we'll talk about adding another medication , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your second problem is your hypertension . that seems to be relatively stable . i want you to stay on your current medication . you're on li- lisinopril 10 mg once a day . just stay right on that medication . no changes at this point . seems to be doing well , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . anything else i can do for you today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . come with me , we'll get you checked out .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 24-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes and hypertension.
Annual exam
The patient started working at a bakery and reports increased snacking on sweets.
Diabetes
The doctor asks about the patient's blood sugar levels.
The patient reports uncontrolled blood sugars due to diet and confirms adherence to medication.
Hypertension
The doctor asks about the patient's blood pressure and blood sugar monitoring.
The patient reports stable blood pressure despite fluctuating blood sugars.
The doctor asks if the patient is sleeping well.
The patient reports sleeping 8 hours per night.
The doctor asks about suicidal ideation or self-harm.
The patient denies suicidal ideation or self-harm.
The doctor initiates a physical examination.
Physical exam findings include a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, intact neurovascular status, and no carotid bruits, thyromegaly, or edema.
The patient's hemoglobin A1c is elevated.
The doctor asks if the patient is still taking metformin.
The doctor asks about the frequency of metformin administration.
The patient confirms taking metformin twice a day.
The doctor advises the patient to reduce snacking and sweets.
The doctor instructs the patient to check fasting blood sugars, call in results in two weeks, and return for follow-up in two months.
The patient is instructed to continue lisinopril 10 mg once daily.
